what_it_does: Implements an MCP server named company-channel that also runs a local HTTP listener; it registers session metadata under .data/channels/, forwards inbound POSTs into the active Claude Code session as notifications/claude/channel, and exposes reply/announce/profile tools for two-way fabric messaging.
observations: Combines stdio MCP transport with an HTTP server on a configurable or random local port.
Writes a JSON registration file per handle in .data/channels/ containing handle, session_id, cwd, description, model, profile, pid, claude_pid, port, and start time.
Computes a shared session key by walking /proc ancestors to find the claude process PID.
Provides three MCP tools: reply (POSTs to supervisor /channel-reply), announce (one-line description), and profile (self-written model/role/focus/expertise).
Coerces every inbound meta value to string and drops null/undefined to avoid silent notification failures.
Uses best-effort HTTP calls to the supervisor and silently swallows registry update errors.
Cleans up its registration file on exit, SIGINT, and SIGTERM.
standouts: The /proc-based claude ancestor PID detector used as a session-unique key.
The choke-point meta string-coercion guard with a dated provenance comment.
The dual role as both MCP server and HTTP ingress point for live conversation injection.
conventions: Environment-variable configuration with sensible fallbacks for handle, session id, port, repo root, and supervisor base.
Filesystem-based registry under .data/channels/ with one JSON file per channel.
Best-effort error handling: postSupervisor resolves on error, updateReg catches and returns false.
Inline design/issue comments with dates and ticket numbers.
ES module syntax with top-level await.
concerns: claudeAncestorPid parses /proc, which is Linux-specific and brittle if process names or cmdline contain spaces or parentheses.
PORT defaults to 0, so the actual port is random and discoverable only via the registry.
postSupervisor resolves on any error, hiding delivery failures from the caller.
updateReg silently ignores read/write/JSON errors, so profile/announce may appear to succeed while the disk entry is stale.
Signal handlers call process.exit immediately without draining the HTTP server or MCP transport.
Inbound POST body is accumulated as a string with no length limit or timeout.
safeMeta converts booleans to strings and drops null, which may change semantics for consumers.
notes: This is the local ingress/egress bridge that lets an external supervisor push messages into a live Claude Code session and pull replies back out.
questions: What is the exact supervisor API contract for /channel-reply and other endpoints?
How is the experimental claude/channel/permission capability used?
How are port collisions or multiple channels for the same session handled?
What notification schema does the MCP client expect for notifications/claude/channel?
Does the /proc PID walk work on macOS or other non-Linux platforms?
How do MODEL (env) and profile.model (agent-written) interact or conflict?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it registers a channel, receives external POSTs, injects them as notifications, and routes replies back through the supervisor.
apparent_themes: Platform-specific process introspection for session correlation.
intention_signals: It appears designed to turn individual Claude Code sessions into nodes on a local operator/supervisor fabric, where each node can be discovered by handle, receive injected instructions, and reply without leaving the conversation.
novelty: Distinctive elements include using the claude process ancestor PID as a session-unique correlation key, running an HTTP listener inside an MCP server to inject notifications, and the explicit meta string-coercion guard against silent notification loss.
contribution: It contributes a concrete two-way channel bridge: MCP registration plus HTTP ingress, a per-session JSON registry, reply routing to a supervisor, and self-announcement/profile tools that let a session publish its identity and role to the fabric.
summary_for_embedding: company_channel.mjs is a Node.js MCP server and local HTTP listener that registers a Claude Code session in a filesystem channel registry, forwards inbound POSTs as conversation notifications, and provides reply/announce/profile tools for cross-session fabric messaging.
intention_for_embedding: The file exists to let an external supervisor discover and inject live messages into a Claude Code session and carry replies back to the originating session.

symbols.does: Walks /proc ancestors to find the claude process PID used as a session key.
symbols.description: Reads /proc/<pid>/stat and cmdline for up to 40 generations, matching comm 'claude', executable base 'claude', or a version-number comm; returns the matching PID or null. It is Linux-specific and intentionally failure-isolated.

symbols.does: Builds the JSON object describing this channel session.
symbols.description: Collects handle, session_id, cwd, description, model, profile, process pid, discovered claude_pid, listening port, and ISO start timestamp into the registry entry structure.

symbols.does: Creates .data/channels/ and writes the initial registration JSON file.
symbols.description: Ensures the registry directory exists and writes the full channel entry to .data/channels/<handle>.json when the HTTP server starts.

symbols.does: Merges a patch into the existing on-disk registration file.
symbols.description: Reads the current registry file, applies Object.assign with the supplied patch, and writes it back; returns true on success and false on any error, preserving transport fields written by the server.

symbols.does: POSTs a JSON payload to a supervisor HTTP endpoint.
symbols.description: Best-effort HTTP POST to SUPERVISOR + pathname with JSON body; resolves on response end or on any error, so failures are not propagated to MCP tool callers.

symbols.does: Deletes the channel's registry file on shutdown.
symbols.description: Unlinks .data/channels/<handle>.json; registered for process exit, SIGINT, and SIGTERM to remove stale channel entries.
